verificationLabel: Round 7 team lists checked Tuesday 14 Apr 2026
summary: Round 7 has moved out of placeholder mode now that official team lists are out, with the early edge sitting around Canterbury against Parramatta while several other games still need price discipline.

scoreboard.note: Leo Thompson is back for Canterbury while Parramatta lose more pack depth and are still reeling from the Titans blowout.
Nathan Cleary is named and Penrith still look steadier than a Dolphins side that remains light on depth.
The Cowboys lose Murray Taulagi and Reed Mahoney from last week, while Manly get Taukeiaho back on the bench.
Dylan Brown returns for Newcastle, but the Roosters still look like the cleaner, steadier side.
Kurt Capewell returns, with Charnze Nicoll-Klokstad and Mitch Barnett also hovering in the reserves.
Jarome Luai is back for the Tigers, while Brisbane still carry a wounded roster even with Reynolds named.
Jye Gray is out and Matt Dufty comes in, but Souths still look healthier than a Dragons side missing Moses Suli.
Canberra kept rolling and Melbourne now have to prove they can be trusted after another heavy loss.

liveAlerts.text: Official Round 7 team lists are now out, so the board can move from broad setup into sharper reads.
The running charity profit stays at $11.50, so there is still no pressure to force an early play.
Jye Gray is out for South Sydney, while the Cowboys lose Murray Taulagi and Reed Mahoney from their derby win.
Jarome Luai is back for the Tigers and Kurt Capewell returns for the Warriors.
Eels, Storm and Dragons all still sit in the prove-it bucket heading into Round 7.
Bulldogs over Eels is the strongest early shortlist play, but the charity bet should stay open until the market settles.
